project.name: Knotz Raven Mayhem
project.productGoal: Continue expanding the Phaser arcade shooter into a fuller, deployable browser game with more gameplay content, stronger between-stage and end-game flows, richer weapon feedback, and tighter pacing.
project.v1ScopeDecision: Local records and local progression only. Online leaderboards and account features are deferred beyond the first public release.

currentState.summary: The original raven click-target prototype has been rebuilt into a Phaser 3 + TypeScript + Vite arcade shooter with staged runs, bosses, armory progression, persistent local saves, DOM overlays, procedural stage art, stage-aware procedural audio, release verification scripts, and GitHub Actions workflow scaffolding.

implementedContent.stages: 6 named stages with different spawn pools, palettes, speed multipliers, rewards, procedural set dressing, and overflow scaling after the final named stage.
implementedContent.enemies: 9 enemy definitions: normal, fast, golden, armored, mini, shield, splitter, dive, and boss.
implementedContent.weapons: 4 guns: Quarter Pistol, Burst Rifle, Scattergun, Arc Laser.
implementedContent.crosshairs: 4 current crosshair unlocks: Classic Sight, Neon Dot, Eagle Eye, Wide Net. These are still treated like purchasable loadout items and should be revisited per the new direction below.
implementedContent.upgrades: 4 permanent upgrade tracks: cooldown, combo window, starting lives, payout scaling.
implementedContent.powerups: 5 powerups: slow-mo, multi-shot, score boost, extra life, overdrive.
implementedContent.audio: Procedural WebAudio with menu/run/boss modes, stage-aware motifs, weapon-specific shots, enemy-specific hit cues, powerup cues, boss warning/defeat stings, stage clear, miss, and game over.
implementedContent.visuals: Seed raven/explosion assets plus procedural stage set dressing, mobile particle caps, stronger death sequence, and cabinet-style HUD framing.
implementedContent.releaseOps: Release verification scripts, balance report, served dist smoke, GitHub Actions CI, and manual GitHub Pages workflow are in place.

knownUnfinishedAreas: Manual play-session balance is still required.
Final browser/manual QA checklist has not been executed end to end.
Remote GitHub Actions and public GitHub Pages deployment have not been validated from GitHub.
Final original production visuals are still open: raven variants, boss sprite work, richer UI cabinet art, and optional recorded audio.
Mobile tuning was implemented in code but still needs real touch-device validation.

nextTeamPrimaryFocus.theme: Gameplay content expansion and stronger run flow.

priorityOrder.title: Add more gameplay content
details: Add more named stages beyond the current six-stage arc.
Add new enemy archetypes with clear introduction pacing.
Add more powerups and scoring effects that change moment-to-moment play.
Add shader or graphics effects that make hits, combos, boss moments, stage clears, and powerup states feel more dramatic.
Keep new content data-driven through src/game/data where possible.

priorityOrder.title: Add between-stage session UI
details: Create an interstitial UI between stage clears and the next stage start.
Use it to show stage rewards, coins gained, accuracy/combo highlights, next-stage preview, new enemy warnings, and a clear continue action.
This should reduce the current abrupt transition from one stage to the next.

priorityOrder.title: Strengthen end-game flow toward the armory
details: After game over, push players directly toward useful armory actions instead of making them discover the armory from the menu.
Show newly earned coins, affordable upgrades, unlock recommendations, and a prominent Armory action.
Keep Run It Back available, but make progression spending feel like the natural next step.

priorityOrder.title: Rework gun and crosshair identity
details: Crosshairs should become gun-related effects/readability tools, not separate skins players buy.
Gun effects should drive the crosshair look and behavior.
Evaluate whether current purchasable crosshairs should be removed, folded into weapons, or converted into weapon mods.

priorityOrder.title: Improve multi-bullet and shotgun feel
details: Burst Rifle and Scattergun need more exaggerated animations.
Brainstorm larger/dynamic crosshairs, visible pellets, short-lived bullet traces, muzzle fan effects, or impact ripples.
Make multi-shot and shotgun behavior obvious at a glance without cluttering the playfield.

priorityOrder.title: Add bonus levels
details: Prototype bonus stages that break the standard survival rhythm.
Possible patterns: golden raven jackpot rounds, target-gallery streak rounds, no-miss precision rounds, powerup frenzy rounds, or boss-vulnerability bonus windows.
Decide whether bonus levels are scheduled after specific stages, triggered by performance, or purchased/unlocked from progression.

priorityOrder.title: Convey gun cooldowns more vividly
details: Current cooldown is mechanically enforced but not vivid enough.
Add weapon-specific cooldown meters, crosshair recharge animation, muzzle/cabinet light, or click feedback when firing too early.
Make cooldown readable on desktop and mobile.

priorityOrder.title: Tighten difficulty pacing and enemy introductions
details: Use DOCS/BALANCE-NOTES.md and npm run balance:report as the numeric baseline.
Ensure new enemy types are introduced with readable first encounters before being mixed into harder pools.
Tune boss health, stage pressure, powerup drop rates, stage length, rewards, and unlock timing from real play sessions.

currentBacklogFromDocs: Finish or replace final production visuals: raven variants, boss sprite work, richer UI cabinet art, optional recorded music/SFX.
Validate economy and difficulty from real play sessions.
Validate mobile-specific tuning on real touch devices.
Run remote GitHub Actions and manual GitHub Pages deploy workflow before public release.

recommendedFirstSprint.reason: It improves pacing immediately, creates a natural place to introduce new enemies/stages, and supports future bonus levels.
acceptance: Stage clear pauses into a DOM interstitial instead of immediately starting the next stage.
Interstitial shows reward, stage stats, next stage title/subtitle, and any new enemy/boss warning.
Continue action starts the next stage cleanly.

recommendedFirstSprint.reason: It strengthens the core progression loop and makes coins feel valuable.
acceptance: Game-over screen prominently shows coins earned and affordable upgrades/unlocks.
Primary action routes to Armory/Menu or a direct armory mode instead of leaving discovery to the player.
Run It Back remains available as a secondary action.
Returning to menu preserves updated save and records.

recommendedFirstSprint.reason: It addresses several requested items together: gun-specific crosshair identity, bullets/traces, shotgun/multibullet readability, and cooldown communication.
acceptance: Each weapon has distinct aim/cooldown feedback.
Burst and scatter shots show visible multi-projectile or trace effects.
Cooldown state is readable without firing repeatedly.
Crosshair behavior follows equipped gun identity rather than purchasable cosmetic skin identity.

architecturePointers.runtime: src/main.ts creates Phaser game with BootScene, AttractScene, and GameScene.
architecturePointers.gameplayScene: src/game/scenes/GameScene.ts owns active run spawning, input, hit resolution, stage flow, boss flow, background set dressing, effects, and HUD dispatch.
architecturePointers.menuScene: src/game/scenes/AttractScene.ts owns attract screen state, armory/records/options/credits commands, save mutations, and menu music.
architecturePointers.uiLayer: src/ui/app.ts renders DOM overlays for attract, armory, records, options, HUD, pause, and game over. src/ui/events.ts is the scene/UI command bridge.
architecturePointers.data: src/game/data/stages.ts, enemies.ts, weapons.ts, upgrades.ts, tuning.ts are the main data/tuning entry points.
architecturePointers.save: src/game/save.ts normalizes localStorage save data and applies purchases/rewards.
architecturePointers.balance: scripts/report-balance.mjs reads live TypeScript data and estimates stage pacing, rewards, and economy costs.
architecturePointers.releaseChecks: scripts/check-release.mjs validates dist files. scripts/smoke-dist.mjs serves dist from /KnotzBirdShot/ and fetches linked assets.

manualValidationStillRequired: Manual GitHub Pages deployment through the Deploy GitHub Pages workflow_dispatch event

recommendedSubagents.name: Content Expansion Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Stages, enemies, powerups, scoring effects, bonus levels, and content pacing.

recommendedSubagents.name: Mobile Performance Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Real-device touch feel, HUD density, performance caps, and mobile readability.

recommendedSubagents.name: Run Flow And Progression Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Between-stage UI, stronger game-over-to-armory flow, save-safe progression surfaces.

recommendedSubagents.name: Combat Feel And Weapon FX Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Gun identity, crosshair redesign, projectile/tracer feedback, shotgun and multibullet effects, cooldown readability.

recommendedSubagents.name: Release QA And Deployment Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Release gates, GitHub Pages Actions deployment, published-build validation, and final checklist tracking.

githubPagesDeploymentNote.why: The classic Pages branch/folder source can show a blank white screen after the Vite migration because it may serve stale or mismatched static output. The Actions deployment uploads the freshly built dist artifact.

implementationGuidance: Keep gameplay rules and tunables in data/system modules where possible instead of burying values in scene code.
Use DOM overlays for text-heavy UI such as between-stage summaries, armory prompts, upgrade recommendations, and end-game progression prompts.
Use Phaser graphics/sprites for playfield effects, projectile traces, impact bursts, crosshair animation, and stage/boss presentation.
When save shape changes, update normalization in src/game/save.ts and test returning-save behavior manually.
After content or tuning changes, rerun npm run balance:report and update DOCS/BALANCE-NOTES.md if the baseline changes materially.
After runtime, asset, or release-script changes, run npm run release:verify before handoff.
openDesignQuestions: Should current purchasable crosshairs be removed from the economy, converted into weapon mods, or preserved as accessibility/readability options?
Should bonus levels be deterministic after certain stages or performance-triggered?
How many stages should the first public content target include beyond the current six?
Should stage interstitials allow armory purchases between stages, or should armory remain between runs only?
What is the desired full-armory completion time for an average player?
